other_observers: I was leading an AVAS group. Brandon Percival was near me at the time but did not get onto the bird and it only showerd once.;
specific_location: Bent's Old Fort , north end of the cattail marsh.
description: Size and shape indicated that this was either a Marsh Wren or Sedge Wren. In direct comparison to Marsh Wrens seen directly before and after this birds sighting , had a streaked crown not a flat brown crown , indistinct white supercilium after the eye , buffy flanks , some black and buffy barring on the wings , and a shorter tail.Behaviors: Even more skulking than the Marsh Wrens. perched briefly on a cattail and then flew very short distance into denser cattails. No response to pishing or playing tape after this initial sighting.Call: None givenPlumage: Adult
